I'm here to tell you the story 'bout a robot named Carl. He came from a far away land called Factory. Carl was sad 'cause he was missing a part called a tire. He also needed a sun gatherer. But, the tire was more important. Once Carl got all these parts he could travel to his new home in the nation of Lab and the city of Office. It was a tricky thing to get there with missing parts. Just as he had given up hope Carl got a message from Mr. X saying the new parts were ready to be delivered. This made the robot very happy. The parts arrived a few days later and Carl put them in with 2 days of work. After this Carl began to travel the last bit of his goal to get to his new job. After this Carl took 10 days to get to Lab. What Carl need 'fore he went to the lab?

Calaveras Kate, a saloon singer who's in love with Jason, is relieved when the stranger decides not to shoot. Rafael Moreno suddenly rides into town and starts beef with the stranger. They keep fighting till Judge Wallace Wintrop and his niece, Sheila, who just got here from back East, show up and ain't happy about all the violence out West. The stranger turns out to be Reb Randall, the dead man's brother. He's on the hunt for the killer, who might be Rafael, or Jason, or even Billy Buckett, the coward of the county. The women are holding their breath, waiting to see if the men they love will make it through. Which women holdin' they breath?

Zakariya Essabar, a Moroccan citizen, moved to Germany in February 1997 and to Hamburg in 1998, where he studied medical technology. Soon after moving to Hamburg, Essabar met Binalshibh and the others through a Turkish mosque. Essabar turned extremist fairly suddenly, probably in 1999, and reportedly pressured one acquaintance with physical force to become more religious, grow a beard, and compel his wife to convert to Islam. Essabar's parents were said to have made repeated but unsuccessful efforts to sway him from this lifestyle. Shortly before the 9/11 attacks, he would travel to Afghanistan to communicate the date for the attacks to the al Qaeda leadership. Mounir el Motassadeq, another Moroccan, came to Germany in 1993, moving to Hamburg two years later to study electrical engineering at theTechnical University. A witness has recalled Motassadeq saying that he would kill his entire family if his religious beliefs demanded it. One of Motassadeq's roommates recalls him referring to Hitler as a "good man" and organizing film sessions that included speeches by  Bin Laden. Motassadeq would help conceal the Hamburg group's trip to Afghanistan in late 1999. Abdelghani Mz Which country did Essabar and Binalshibh link up in?

When he got back to Nuremberg in 1495, Durer set up his own shop (had to be married to do that). Over the next five years, his art started mixing in Italian styles with the Northern styles he knew. Durer's daddy passed away in 1502, and his mama in 1513. The best stuff he made early on in his shop were his woodcut prints, mostly about religion, but he also did scenes like The Men's Bath House (around 1496). These cuts were bigger and finer than most German woodcuts before, and they were way more complex and balanced in how they were set up. Folks now think Durer probably didn't cut any of the woodblocks himself; a specialist craftsman would have done that. But, his training in Wolgemut's studio, where they made a lot of carved and painted altarpieces and did the design and cutting for woodcuts, really taught him a lot about what you could do with the technique and how to work with the folks who cut the blocks. Durer either drew his design straight on the woodblock, or he stuck a paper drawing on the block. Either way, his drawings got messed up when they cut the block. Whether dem designs was drawn straight on da block or was paper drawings glued to da block, what was da end result of da original work?
